part.text:
# Batch Invoice Extraction with Bounded Concurrency (LlamaCloud TS SDK)

## Background
You are building a production batch-extraction pipeline that pulls structured fields out of a folder of invoice PDFs using **LlamaCloud Extract v2** through the TypeScript SDK `@llamaindex/llama-cloud`.

The pipeline must scale to many files without overwhelming the LlamaCloud API. You will:
- Upload files to LlamaCloud,
- Run schema-driven extraction jobs **concurrently but with a bounded worker pool** (so you do not blow past your account's rate limits),
- Poll each job until it reaches a terminal status, with a per-job client-side timeout,
- Persist a structured result document for every input PDF (including failures),
- Print a final summary so the script can be wired into CI.

## Requirements
- Implement the pipeline in a **single TypeScript file** that runs end-to-end via `npx tsx run.ts`.
- Discover every `*.pdf` file (case-insensitive) under `/home/user/myproject/invoices/` and process them as a batch.
- Use a **Zod schema** named conceptually `Invoice` covering at least: `vendor_name` (string), `invoice_number` (string), `total_amount` (number), `currency` (3-letter ISO code string).
- Pass that Zod schema to LlamaCloud Extract by converting it to a JSON Schema with `z.toJSONSchema(...)`.
- Use the `cost_effective` tier and `per_doc` extraction target.
- Limit in-flight extract jobs to **at most 3 concurrent** workers (use any concurrency primitive you like, e.g. `p-limit`).
- Apply a **per-job timeout of 240 seconds** to the polling loop; if a job is still not in a terminal status after the timeout elapses, treat it as a failure (do not throw and abort the whole batch).
- Write a JSON artifact to `/home/user/myproject/results.json` that is a JSON array. Each element corresponds to one input PDF and has the shape (`data` is present on success, `error` is present on failure — never both):

  ```json
  {
    "file": "<basename of the input pdf, e.g. invoice_acme.pdf>",
    "status": "success" | "error",
    "data": { "vendor_name": "...", "invoice_number": "...", "total_amount": 1234.56, "currency": "USD" },
    "error": "<error message string>"
  }
  ```

- Write a human-readable log to `/home/user/myproject/output.log` containing at minimum one line per file in the form `PROCESSED <file>: <status>` and a final summary line in the form `SUMMARY total=<N> success=<S> failed=<F>` (also printed to stdout).

## Implementation Hints
- Authenticate the client with the `LLAMA_CLOUD_API_KEY` environment variable (already set in the environment).
- Upload each file with `client.files.create({ file: fs.createReadStream(path), purpose: 'extract' })` to get a `dfl-...` file id.
- Create extract jobs with `client.extract.create({ file_input: <file_id>, configuration: { data_schema: ..., extraction_target: 'per_doc', tier: 'cost_effective' } })` and poll with `client.extract.get(jobId)` until `job.status` is one of `COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, or `CANCELLED`.
- A successful job exposes the extracted data on `job.extract_result`.
- Order of entries in `results.json` does not matter — the verifier matches by `file`.
- A `node_modules/` install is **not** preinstalled. Create whatever `package.json` you need and install the dependencies you choose.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
- Ensure the script is actually executed against the real LlamaCloud API and the artifacts exist.
- Log file: `/home/user/myproject/output.log`
- Output file: `/home/user/myproject/results.json`
- The pipeline must process every `*.pdf` (case-insensitive) under `/home/user/myproject/invoices/` and emit exactly one entry per file in `results.json`.
- Each `results.json` entry must follow the schema in the Requirements section. Successful entries must contain `data.vendor_name`, `data.invoice_number`, `data.total_amount` (number), and `data.currency` (string).
- The log file must contain a final summary line matching the regex `^SUMMARY total=\d+ success=\d+ failed=\d+$`.
- The same summary line must also be printed to stdout when the script runs.
- The TypeScript source must invoke the official `@llamaindex/llama-cloud` v2 SDK (i.e. `import ... from '@llamaindex/llama-cloud'`) — the verifier will grep the source for this import.
- The TypeScript source must contain a Zod schema (i.e. `import ... from 'zod'`) — the verifier will grep the source for this import.
- The extraction must use the `cost_effective` tier (the string `'cost_effective'` must appear in the source).

# Batch Invoice Extraction with Bounded Concurrency (LlamaCloud TS SDK)

## Background
You are building a production batch-extraction pipeline that pulls structured fields out of a folder of invoice PDFs using **LlamaCloud Extract v2** through the TypeScript SDK `@llamaindex/llama-cloud`.

The pipeline must scale to many files without overwhelming the LlamaCloud API. You will:
- Upload files to LlamaCloud,
- Run schema-driven extraction jobs **concurrently but with a bounded worker pool** (so you do not blow past your account's rate limits),
- Poll each job until it reaches a terminal status, with a per-job client-side timeout,
- Persist a structured result document for every input PDF (including failures),
- Print a final summary so the script can be wired into CI.

## Requirements
- Implement the pipeline in a **single TypeScript file** that runs end-to-end via `npx tsx run.ts`.
- Discover every `*.pdf` file (case-insensitive) under `/home/user/myproject/invoices/` and process them as a batch.
- Use a **Zod schema** named conceptually `Invoice` covering at least: `vendor_name` (string), `invoice_number` (string), `total_amount` (number), `currency` (3-letter ISO code string).
- Pass that Zod schema to LlamaCloud Extract by converting it to a JSON Schema with `z.toJSONSchema(...)`.
- Use the `cost_effective` tier and `per_doc` extraction target.
- Limit in-flight extract jobs to **at most 3 concurrent** workers (use any concurrency primitive you like, e.g. `p-limit`).
- Apply a **per-job timeout of 240 seconds** to the polling loop; if a job is still not in a terminal status after the timeout elapses, treat it as a failure (do not throw and abort the whole batch).
- Write a JSON artifact to `/home/user/myproject/results.json` that is a JSON array. Each element corresponds to one input PDF and has the shape (`data` is present on success, `error` is present on failure — never both):

  ```json
  {
    "file": "<basename of the input pdf, e.g. invoice_acme.pdf>",
    "status": "success" | "error",
    "data": { "vendor_name": "...", "invoice_number": "...", "total_amount": 1234.56, "currency": "USD" },
    "error": "<error message string>"
  }
  ```

- Write a human-readable log to `/home/user/myproject/output.log` containing at minimum one line per file in the form `PROCESSED <file>: <status>` and a final summary line in the form `SUMMARY total=<N> success=<S> failed=<F>` (also printed to stdout).

## Implementation Hints
- Authenticate the client with the `LLAMA_CLOUD_API_KEY` environment variable (already set in the environment).
- Upload each file with `client.files.create({ file: fs.createReadStream(path), purpose: 'extract' })` to get a `dfl-...` file id.
- Create extract jobs with `client.extract.create({ file_input: <file_id>, configuration: { data_schema: ..., extraction_target: 'per_doc', tier: 'cost_effective' } })` and poll with `client.extract.get(jobId)` until `job.status` is one of `COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, or `CANCELLED`.
- A successful job exposes the extracted data on `job.extract_result`.
- Order of entries in `results.json` does not matter — the verifier matches by `file`.
- A `node_modules/` install is **not** preinstalled. Create whatever `package.json` you need and install the dependencies you choose.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
- Ensure the script is actually executed against the real LlamaCloud API and the artifacts exist.
- Log file: `/home/user/myproject/output.log`
- Output file: `/home/user/myproject/results.json`
- The pipeline must process every `*.pdf` (case-insensitive) under `/home/user/myproject/invoices/` and emit exactly one entry per file in `results.json`.
- Each `results.json` entry must follow the schema in the Requirements section. Successful entries must contain `data.vendor_name`, `data.invoice_number`, `data.total_amount` (number), and `data.currency` (string).
- The log file must contain a final summary line matching the regex `^SUMMARY total=\d+ success=\d+ failed=\d+$`.
- The same summary line must also be printed to stdout when the script runs.
- The TypeScript source must invoke the official `@llamaindex/llama-cloud` v2 SDK (i.e. `import ... from '@llamaindex/llama-cloud'`) — the verifier will grep the source for this import.
- The TypeScript source must contain a Zod schema (i.e. `import ... from 'zod'`) — the verifier will grep the source for this import.
- The extraction must use the `cost_effective` tier (the string `'cost_effective'` must appear in the source).

The batch invoice extraction pipeline has been implemented and executed successfully.

## What was built

**`/home/user/myproject/run.ts`** — single TypeScript file that:
- Discovers all `*.pdf` files under `/home/user/myproject/invoices/` (found: `acme.pdf`, `globex.pdf`, `initech.pdf`)
- Defines a **Zod v4 schema** (`Invoice`) with `vendor_name`, `invoice_number`, `total_amount`, `currency`
- Converts it to JSON Schema via `z.toJSONSchema(Invoice)`
- Uploads each PDF via `client.files.create()` using `toFile()` to wrap the Node.js read stream
- Submits extraction jobs via `client.extract.create()` with `tier: 'cost_effective'` and `extraction_target: 'per_doc'`
- Uses **`p-limit(3)`** to cap concurrency at 3 in-flight jobs
- Polls `client.extract.get(jobId)` with a **240-second per-job client-side timeout**; timed-out/failed jobs become error entries (no batch abort)
- Writes structured **`/home/user/myproject/results.json`** and **`/home/user/myproject/output.log`**

**`/home/user/myproject/package.json`** — declares `@llamaindex/llama-cloud@^2.0.0`, `zod@4`, `p-limit@^6.2.0`, `tsx`, `typescript`.
